content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

April 21, 1997

Lori O. Lim
Department of Pesticide Regulation
California Environmental Protection Agency
1020 N Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-97-154

Dear Dr. Lim:

This letter is a response to your request for clarification of advice provided to you
regarding the honoraria provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

QUESTION

Are you required to report as income honoraria received by you from a source which you
are not required to report on your statement of economic interests?

CONCLUSION

No. You are only required to report income from a source that your agency's conflict of
interest code requires to be reported on your statement of economic interests. However, if you
receive $250 or more from that source, you may not make, participate in the making, or
otherwise use your official position to influence a governmental decision that will have a
reasonably foreseeable and material financial affect on that source of income.

FACTS

You are a designated employee of the Department of Pesticide Regulation
("Department"), a state agency. You presently serve as a staff toxicologist, and have recently
been invited to write a review article for the journal Pesticide Outlook. Pesticide Outlook is a
bi-monthly publication produced by the Royal Society of Chemistry ("Society") in Cambridge,

¹ Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 -
18995, of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 (916) 322-5660

L

File No. A-97-154
Page 2

England. It publishes reviews (usually invited) and articles of general interest to pesticide
professionals. Pesticide Outlook has proposed an honorarium of 30 British pounds/page to be
paid by the Society. You were advised in the Lim Advice Letter, No. A-97-070, that the receipt
of payment for writing the article would not constitute a prohibited honorarium.

You are not required to report income from the Society on your statement of economic
interests. Your disclosure category in the Department's conflict of interest code requires you to
report "all business positions in business entities, investments, and sources of income from
businesses which hold, or during the reporting period held, or was an applicant for, any license,
permit, certificate, or registration issued by the Department."

To your knowledge, the Society does not hold and has not applied for any licenses,
permits, certificates, or registrations issued by the Department.

ANALYSIS

Section 89502(c) provides that no designated employee of a state agency shall accept an
honorarium from any source if the employee would be required to report the receipt of income or
gifts from that source on his or her statement of economic interests.

You are a designated employee of the Department of Pesticide Regulation. Your
disclosure category in the Department's conflict of interest code requires you to report "all
business positions in business entities, investments, and sources of income from businesses
which hold, or during the reporting period held, or was an applicant for, any license, permit,
certificate, or registration issued by the Department."

To your knowledge, the Society does not hold and has not applied for any licenses,
permits, certificates, or registrations issued by the Department. If that is the case, the Society is
not a source you would be required to report on your statement of economic interests. Therefore,
as advised in the Lim Advice Letter, supra, you may accept the payments from the Society
without violating the honoraria ban in Section 89502(c). In addition, you are not required to
report the payment on your statement of economic interests.

Please keep in mind, however, that once you receive income from the Society, it will be
considered a source of income to you. Section 87100 of the Act prohibits any public official
from making, participating in making, or otherwise using his or her official position to influence
a governmental decision in which the official has a financial interest. (Gammon Advice Letter,
No. A-96-340.) A financial interest includes a source of income of $250 or more in the
preceding twelve months. (Section 87103(c).) Thus, although the payments are not reportable,
if one of your decisions at the Department will materially financially affect the Society, you must
disqualify yourself from that decision.

File No. A-97-154
Page 3

This advice is limited to the provisions of the Act. You should contact your agency for
assistance on any possible incompatible activities issue. If you have any other questions
regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: Luisa Menchaca
Senior Staff Counsel, Legal Division

SGC:LM:ak
